question_text: A kettle has a power rating of 2400 W and takes 150 seconds to boil water. A microwave has a power rating of 800 W and takes 300 seconds to heat the same amount of water. Which statement is correct?
options: The kettle transfers more energy because it has higher power
The microwave transfers more energy because it runs for longer
Both devices transfer exactly the same amount of energy
The kettle transfers 360,000 J while the microwave transfers 240,000 J
correct_answer: The kettle transfers 360,000 J while the microwave transfers 240,000 J
explanation: Energy = Power × Time. Kettle: 2400 W × 150 s = 360,000 J. Microwave: 800 W × 300 s = 240,000 J. Higher power over shorter time transfers more total energy than lower power over longer time.
